scenario_title: Delete non-existent RRset after passing prereq
test_rationale: The zone contains a single helper owner name that is demonstrably in use via an A RRset, satisfying the update prerequisite while leaving the TXT RRset absent. The UPDATE then deletes that absent TXT RRset, which is the underspecified state-dependent case. A follow-up TXT QUERY provides a stable post-state check: the RRset should remain absent in all cases, so the observable divergence is primarily the UPDATE reply code.

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rrset for an already-absent RRset as a successful no-op when prerequisites pass.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE because the targeted RRset is absent, despite the prerequisite passing.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.1–§3.4.2 defines RRset deletion syntax and general update processing, but does not precisely specify the required outcome when delete_rrset targets an RRset that is already absent after all prerequisites have succeeded. Implementations may therefore differ on whether to return NOERROR as a harmless no-op or NXRRSET/SERVFAIL as an error.
